summary: This is a brief blog post linking to Joel's Inc. magazine column about why Circuit City failed while B&H Photo thrives. Joel celebrates honest businesses succeeding over dishonest competitors, expressing satisfaction at seeing B&H's loyal customer base grow while Circuit City went bankrupt. The post is essentially a teaser pointing readers to the full column.
key_points: B&H Photo thrives with loyal customers while competitors like Circuit City have gone bankrupt
Honest dealing is presented as a sustainable competitive advantage over deceptive business practices
Joel frames Circuit City's failure as poetic justice for dishonest retail practices
money_quotes: For a business owner, there's nothing more satisfying than watching honest dealers expand their operations while the schmucks, with their going-out-of-business markups, go down the drain.
key_insight: Businesses built on honest dealing and customer loyalty will outlast competitors who rely on deceptive practices.
